elements: Devised or participated in a scheme to defraud
Used interstate wire communications in furtherance of the scheme
Had intent to defraud
Obtained money or property through the scheme
notes: Sentences enhanced if targeting financial institution (up to 30 years, $1M fine).

elements: Devised a scheme to defraud
Used US mail or private carrier in furtherance
Had intent to defraud
notes: One of the most commonly charged federal offenses.

elements: Tax deficiency existed
Affirmative act of evasion
Willfulness (knowing and intentional violation)
notes: Failure to file is a misdemeanor (26 USC 7203). Tax fraud includes civil penalties.

elements: Knowingly executed a scheme to defraud a financial institution
Obtained money, assets, credits, or property from the institution
Did so by means of false pretenses, representations, or promises
notes: Applies to federally insured institutions.

elements: Knowingly transferred, possessed, or used
Without lawful authority
A means of identification of another person
In connection with another federal crime
notes: Aggravated identity theft adds mandatory 2 years consecutive. No probation permitted.

elements: Conducted a financial transaction
Involving proceeds of specified unlawful activity
Knew the property involved represented proceeds of unlawful activity
Intended to promote the unlawful activity or conceal the nature/source of proceeds
notes: Fine may be twice the amount laundered. Often charged alongside fraud.

elements: Knowingly or intentionally possessed
A controlled substance
Without a valid prescription
notes: First offense. Subsequent offenses increase penalties. Federal drug scheduling applies.

elements: Knowingly or intentionally manufactured, distributed, or dispensed
A controlled substance
Or possessed with intent to distribute
notes: Mandatory minimums based on drug type and quantity. Life imprisonment possible for large quantities.

elements: Agreement between two or more persons
To commit a federal crime or defraud the United States
At least one overt act in furtherance of the conspiracy
Knowledge of and voluntary participation in the conspiracy
notes: Penalty may equal that of the underlying offense if conspiracy to commit a specific crime.

elements: Forcibly assaulted, resisted, opposed, impeded, intimidated, or interfered with
A federal officer or employee
While the officer was performing official duties
Knew or should have known the person was a federal officer
notes: Up to 20 years if deadly weapon used or serious bodily injury inflicted.

elements: Knowingly possessed a firearm or ammunition
Had been convicted of a crime punishable by more than one year imprisonment
The firearm had traveled in interstate commerce
notes: Enhanced penalties under Armed Career Criminal Act (3+ prior violent felonies/drug offenses = 15-year mandatory minimum).

elements: Knowingly and willfully made
A materially false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation
In a matter within the jurisdiction of the federal government
notes: Covers lying to FBI agents, on federal forms, etc. Applies even without oath.

elements: Corruptly endeavored to influence, obstruct, or impede
The due administration of justice
In a pending judicial proceeding
By threats, force, or corrupt persuasion
notes: Related statutes: witness tampering (1512), contempt (401-402).

elements: Knowingly accessed a protected computer
Without authorization or exceeding authorized access
Obtained information, damaged data, or caused loss
Computer was used in interstate or foreign commerce
notes: Up to 20 years for repeat offenses. Covers hacking, unauthorized access, DDoS attacks.

elements: Unlawfully seized, confined, inveigled, decoyed, kidnapped, abducted, or carried away
A person
For ransom, reward, or otherwise
Traveled in interstate or foreign commerce or used instrumentality thereof
notes: Life imprisonment possible. If victim is under 18, no interstate requirement.

elements: Being an agent of a state, local, or Indian tribal government, or organization receiving federal funds
Embezzled, stole, or intentionally misapplied
Property valued at $5,000 or more
notes: Also 18 USC 656 for bank embezzlement (up to 30 years).

elements: Knowingly made or used any immigration document
That was forged, counterfeited, altered, or falsely made
For the purpose of satisfying immigration requirements
notes: May lead to deportation for non-citizens. Up to 15 years if in furtherance of drug trafficking or terrorism.

elements: Directly or indirectly gave, offered, or promised
Anything of value
To a public official
With intent to influence an official act
notes: Fine may be three times the value of the bribe. Applies to both giver and receiver.

elements: Under oath in a proceeding before a court, grand jury, or other tribunal
Willfully stated or subscribed
Any material matter which the person did not believe to be true
notes: Must be material to the proceeding. Two-witness rule or documentary proof required.

elements: Existence of an enterprise
Engaged in interstate or foreign commerce
Pattern of racketeering activity (at least 2 predicate acts within 10 years)
Defendant conducted or participated in the enterprise through racketeering
notes: Life imprisonment if predicate offense carries life. Asset forfeiture. Civil RICO allows treble damages.

elements: With intent to cause death or serious bodily harm
Took a motor vehicle from the person or presence of another
By force and violence or by intimidation
notes: Up to 25 years if serious bodily injury. Life if death results.

elements: Maliciously damaged or destroyed
By means of fire or an explosive
A building, vehicle, or other property
Used in interstate or foreign commerce
notes: Minimum 5 years if personal injury. Life imprisonment if death results.

elements: Knowingly received, distributed, or possessed
Visual depiction of a minor engaged in sexually explicit conduct
Material traveled in interstate or foreign commerce
notes: Mandatory minimum 5 years (receipt/distribution). Sex offender registration required. Production carries 15-30 years.

elements: Obtained or attempted to obtain property from another
Through use of actual or threatened force, violence, or fear
In a way that affected interstate or foreign commerce
notes: Also covers robbery affecting interstate commerce.

elements: In connection with the purchase or sale of securities
Employed a device, scheme, or artifice to defraud
Made untrue statements or omissions of material fact
Acted with scienter (intent to deceive, manipulate, or defraud)
notes: Institutional penalty up to $25M. Includes insider trading, market manipulation, accounting fraud.

elements: An alien who has been deported or removed
Found in, entered, or attempted to enter the United States
Without proper authorization
notes: Up to 10 years if prior aggravated felony conviction. Up to 20 years if prior drug trafficking or violent felony.

elements: With intent to defraud
Made, forged, or counterfeited
Obligations or securities of the United States
notes: Includes counterfeiting currency, stamps, and other government securities.

elements: Operated or was in actual physical control of a motor vehicle
On federal property (national parks, military bases, etc.)
While under the influence of alcohol or drugs (BAC 0.08+ or impaired)
notes: Handled in federal magistrate court. State DUI laws may also apply.

elements: With intent to kill, injure, harass, intimidate, or surveil
Traveled in interstate or foreign commerce or used mail/electronic communication
Placed a person in reasonable fear of death, serious bodily injury, or substantial emotional distress
notes: Up to 10 years if violation of restraining order. Life if death results.

elements: Willfully caused bodily injury or attempted to do so
Because of actual or perceived race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability
Used fire, firearm, dangerous weapon, or explosive; or the crime affected interstate commerce
notes: Life imprisonment if death results or if attempt to kill. Enhancement for other federal crimes motivated by bias.

elements: Traded securities while in possession of material non-public information
Had a duty to keep the information confidential
Acted with scienter (knowledge that trading was improper)
The information was material (would influence a reasonable investor)
notes: SEC may also seek civil penalties of 3x profits gained or losses avoided. Tippee liability applies.

elements: Knowingly and willfully executed a scheme to defraud
A healthcare benefit program
In connection with the delivery of or payment for healthcare services
notes: Up to 20 years if serious bodily injury results. Life if death results. Exclusion from federal healthcare programs.

elements: Misbehavior in the presence of the court
Disobedience or resistance to a lawful court order
Obstruction of the administration of justice
notes: Civil contempt (coercive) vs. criminal contempt (punitive). Criminal contempt requires jury trial if sentence exceeds 6 months.

elements: Threatened to assault, kidnap, or murder
A federal official, former official, or their family member
With intent to impede, intimidate, or retaliate against the official
In connection with the official's duties
notes: Includes threats against judges, prosecutors, law enforcement. Online threats covered.

elements: Traveled in interstate or foreign commerce
With intent to injure, harass, or intimidate a spouse, intimate partner, or dating partner
Committed or attempted to commit a crime of violence
notes: Life if death results. Also prohibits firearm possession (18 USC 922(g)(8)-(9)).

charge: Failure to Register as Sex Offender
elements: Required to register under SORNA
Traveled in interstate or foreign commerce
Knowingly failed to register or update registration as required
notes: Must have interstate nexus for federal jurisdiction. State registration violations handled in state court.

elements: Possessed, transferred, or made
A firearm covered by the National Firearms Act (machine gun, short-barreled rifle/shotgun, silencer, destructive device)
Without proper registration
notes: Machine gun violations carry mandatory minimum. Asset forfeiture of the weapon.

elements: Knowingly presented or caused to be presented
A false or fraudulent claim for payment
To the United States government
notes: Civil False Claims Act allows treble damages. Criminal penalties under 18 USC 287. Qui tam (whistleblower) provisions.

elements: Knowingly used intimidation, threats, corrupt persuasion, or misleading conduct
Toward another person
With intent to influence, delay, or prevent testimony or evidence
In an official proceeding
notes: Up to 30 years if attempt to kill witness. No requirement that official proceeding be pending.

elements: Transported, transmitted, or transferred in interstate or foreign commerce
Goods, wares, merchandise, securities, or money
Of the value of $5,000 or more
Knowing the same to have been stolen, converted, or taken by fraud
notes: Also covers receiving stolen property (18 USC 2315).

elements: Knowingly or negligently discharged
Pollutants from a point source
Into waters of the United States
Without a required permit or in violation of permit conditions
notes: Knowing violations: up to 6 years, $50K/day. Knowing endangerment: up to 15 years, $250K.

elements: Entered into a contract, combination, or conspiracy
In restraint of trade or commerce
Among the several states or with foreign nations
notes: Corporate fine up to $100M or twice the gain/loss. Covers price-fixing, bid-rigging, market allocation.

elements: Was in the custody of the US or confined in a federal institution
Escaped or attempted to escape from custody or confinement
notes: Up to 10 years if using force or with an unrecovered firearm. Consecutive to existing sentence.

elements: Knowingly possessed
Material containing visual depiction of a minor
Engaged in sexually explicit conduct
Material had been transported in interstate or foreign commerce
notes: Mandatory minimum for prior sex offense conviction. Sex offender registration required.

elements: By force, violence, or intimidation
Took or attempted to take
Money, property, or anything of value
From a person or presence of another
From a bank or credit union
notes: Up to 25 years if armed. Life if death results. Applies to federally insured banks and credit unions.

elements: Knowingly brought, transported, or harbored
An alien in the United States
Knowing or in reckless disregard of the fact that the alien entered or remained illegally
notes: Up to 20 years if for profit. Life if death results. Per-alien penalties.

elements: Caused the death of another person
Through involuntary manslaughter (without malice)
By operating a vehicle while intoxicated
On federal property or in federal jurisdiction
notes: Voluntary manslaughter up to 15 years. State charges more common unless on federal land.

elements: In connection with a bankruptcy case
Knowingly and fraudulently concealed, transferred, or destroyed property
Made a false oath or account, or presented a false claim
notes: Includes concealing assets, making false statements in schedules, bribery of bankruptcy trustee.
